title: 🧹 Code health: Refactor imports and fix lint issues in src/main.py
body:
### 🎯 What:
- Refactored `src/main.py` to use specific imports from `concurrent.futures` (`ThreadPoolExecutor`, `TimeoutError`) instead of the entire module.
- Moved the module-level import `from src.app_runner import AppRunner` to the top of the file to comply with PEP 8 (E402).
- Removed an unnecessary `f` prefix from a static string (F541).

### 💡 Why:
- Improves readability and follows modern Python practices by using explicit imports.
- Resolves pre-existing lint issues identified by `ruff`.
- Ensures the codebase adheres to standard PEP 8 import ordering.

### ✅ Verification:
- Verified that `concurrent.futures` was NOT actually unused (the original issue report was a false positive), so explicit imports were used to maintain functionality.
- Ran `ruff check src/main.py` and confirmed all checks pass.
- Verified that `src/main.py` remains importable and syntactically correct.

### ✨ Result:
- Cleaner, more idiomatic code in the main orchestrator with zero lint warnings.
head: code-health-main-py-imports
